15.title: OAuth login intermittently fails after redirect
15.body: Some users report that logging in via Google/GitHub OAuth sometimes fails right after being redirected back from the provider, with no clear error message. It seems to happen more often when a user starts the login flow, abandons it, and starts a new one before finishing the first. Possibly related to how we handle the `state` param?

commits.message: Add state generation for OAuth authorize URL

22.title: Session tokens never expire under load testing
22.body: During a load test, sessions created hours ago were still being accepted. Expected sessions to expire after token_ttl_seconds.

31.title: Add slugify support for unicode characters
31.body: slugify() currently strips all non a-z0-9 characters, which mangles names with accented characters (e.g. 'Café' becomes 'caf'). Consider transliterating instead of stripping.

47.title: No way to log out -- session tokens are never invalidated on demand
47.body: AuthService supports login() and validate_session(), but there is no logout() or invalidate_session() method. Once a token is issued it stays valid in _sessions until it naturally expires via token_ttl_seconds. Users have no way to explicitly end their session (e.g. on a shared computer), which is a real security gap.

58.title: InMemoryDatabase is not thread-safe under concurrent access
58.body: InMemoryDatabase.put/get/delete operate directly on a plain dict with no locking. Under concurrent requests (e.g. multiple worker threads), this can lead to race conditions -- lost writes, or a KeyError from delete() racing a get(). Needs a lock around the internal _store, or a switch to a thread-safe structure.

63.title: truncate() can split a multi-character symbol at the cutoff point
63.body: truncate() cuts text at a fixed character index (max_length - 1) and appends an ellipsis. For text containing combined character sequences (e.g. an emoji built from multiple code points, or a base character plus a combining accent), cutting mid-sequence can leave a broken, unrenderable fragment right before the ellipsis. Should cut on a grapheme-cluster boundary instead of a raw character index.
